Certificate of Incorporation

Acme AI Limited · Company Registration No. 678901

Company name: Acme AI Limited

Registered office: 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland

Type: Private company limited by shares

Authorised share capital: €50,000 divided into 50,000,000 ordinary shares of €0.001 each and 15,000,000 preferred shares of €0.001 each.

Incorporated on 14 March 2019. Filed with the Companies Registration Office.

Amended and Restated Constitution

Acme AI Limited

Background

(A) Acme AI Limited is a private company limited by shares incorporated in Ireland (Company Registration No. 678901) whose registered office is at 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland.

(B) The Company carries on the business of developing and licensing artificial intelligence software-as-a-service solutions for enterprise customers in the European Union, United Kingdom and North America.

(C) The Company adopted this Constitution following the Series B funding round.

(D) The parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged), the parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Company" means Acme AI Limited References to "Company" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Directors" means the directors of the Company from time to time References to "Directors" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Share" means a share in the capital of the Company References to "Share" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2. Interpretation

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (1) headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; (2) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; (3) references to a person include bodies corporate, unincorporated associations and partnerships; (4) "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901); (5) references to statutes or statutory provisions include any subordinate legislation and any modification, consolidation or re-enactment thereof; (6) references to writing include email where acknowledged by the recipient; (7) references to days are to calendar days unless stated as Business Days; (8) Business Day means a day on which banks are open for business in Dublin, Ireland and London, England; (9) any obligation on a party not to do something includes an obligation not to allow it to be done; (10) the words include and including are without limitation.

The Schedules form part of this Agreement and shall have the same force and effect as if set out in the body of this Agreement. In the event of conflict between the body and a Schedule, the body shall prevail unless the Schedule expressly states otherwise.

This Agreement has been negotiated at arm's length. Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice and that the doctrine of contra proferentem shall not apply in the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

3. Share Capital

The share capital is divided into Ordinary Shares and Preferred Shares with rights as set out in this Constitution.

Preferred Shares carry liquidation preference, anti-dilution, and conversion rights as approved by special resolution on 15 September 2024.

4. Directors

Minimum two, maximum nine directors. Directors may be removed by ordinary resolution.

The Board may delegate day-to-day management to the CEO and executive team.

Register of Directors and Secretaries

Dr. Siobhán O'Connell — Chief Executive Officer — appointed 2019 — address on file

James Murphy — Chief Financial Officer — appointed 2021 — address on file

Elena Vasquez — Chief Technology Officer — appointed 2020 — address on file

Patrick Byrne — Non-Executive Director (Sequoia Nominee) — appointed 2022 — address on file

Fiona Walsh — Non-Executive Director (Independent) — appointed 2023 — address on file

Minutes of Board Meeting

Acme AI Limited · 2024-09-15

MINUTES OF MEETING OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS

COMPANY: Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901)

DATE: 2024-09-15

TIME: 10:00 a.m. GMT

PLACE: 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland (hybrid via Microsoft Teams)

PRESENT: Dr. Siobhán O'Connell (Chair), James Murphy, Elena Vasquez, Patrick Byrne, Fiona Walsh

IN ATTENDANCE: Aoife Ryan (General Counsel), Arthur Cox (External Counsel — for item 4 only)

QUORUM: Present throughout.

1. Chair; Conflicts

Dr. Siobhán O'Connell was appointed Chair. No conflicts of interest were declared except that Patrick Byrne declared his affiliation with Sequoia Capital and abstained from voting on item 5.

2. Minutes of Previous Meeting

The minutes of the meeting held on the prior month were approved without amendment.

3. Management Update

The CEO presented ARR of €18.2m (up 42% YoY), net revenue retention of 118%, and pipeline coverage of 3.1x for Q3. Discussion noted lengthening enterprise sales cycles in DACH region.

CFO reported cash runway of 22 months at current burn and confirmed audit fieldwork on track for April sign-off.

CTO reported successful launch of document intelligence v3.2 and migration of 94% of tenants to new inference cluster.

4. Resolutions

RESOLUTION 1: the Series B funding round be approved

Upon motion duly made and seconded, IT WAS RESOLVED that the series b funding round be approved

RESOLUTION 2: the Constitution be amended

Upon motion duly made and seconded, IT WAS RESOLVED that the constitution be amended

RESOLUTION 3: option pool increase of 4% be approved

Upon motion duly made and seconded, IT WAS RESOLVED that option pool increase of 4% be approved

5. Any Other Business; Close

There being no further business, the Chair declared the meeting closed at 12:14 p.m.

These minutes were approved by the Board on _______________.

……………………………………

Dr. Siobhán O'Connell, Chair

Acme AI Limited · 2025-09-30

MINUTES OF MEETING OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS

COMPANY: Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901)

DATE: 2025-09-30

TIME: 10:00 a.m. GMT

PLACE: 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland (hybrid via Microsoft Teams)

PRESENT: Dr. Siobhán O'Connell (Chair), James Murphy, Elena Vasquez, Patrick Byrne, Fiona Walsh

IN ATTENDANCE: Aoife Ryan (General Counsel), Arthur Cox (External Counsel — for item 4 only)

QUORUM: Present throughout.

1. Chair; Conflicts

Dr. Siobhán O'Connell was appointed Chair. No conflicts of interest were declared except that Patrick Byrne declared his affiliation with Sequoia Capital and abstained from voting on item 5.

2. Minutes of Previous Meeting

The minutes of the meeting held on the prior month were approved without amendment.

3. Management Update

The CEO presented ARR of €18.2m (up 42% YoY), net revenue retention of 118%, and pipeline coverage of 3.1x for Q3. Discussion noted lengthening enterprise sales cycles in DACH region.

CFO reported cash runway of 22 months at current burn and confirmed audit fieldwork on track for April sign-off.

CTO reported successful launch of document intelligence v3.2 and migration of 94% of tenants to new inference cluster.

Shareholders' Agreement

Acme AI Limited · Series B

Parties: Founders, Atlantic Bridge Growth Fund III LP, Sequoia Capital Global Growth Fund IV, Enterprise Ireland Seed & Venture Capital Scheme, and the Company

Background

(A) Acme AI Limited is a private company limited by shares incorporated in Ireland (Company Registration No. 678901) whose registered office is at 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland.

(B) The Company carries on the business of developing and licensing artificial intelligence software-as-a-service solutions for enterprise customers in the European Union, United Kingdom and North America.

(C) The Shareholders wish to regulate their relationship and the management of the Company following the Series B funding round completed on 15 September 2024 in the aggregate amount of €28,500,000.

(D) The parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged), the parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Affiliate" means any entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a party References to "Affiliate" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Board" means the board of directors of the Company References to "Board" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Business Day" means a day when banks are open in Dublin References to "Business Day" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

4.1 "Completion" means completion of the Series B investment References to "Completion" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

5.1 "Drag-Along Notice" means a notice requiring minority shareholders to sell on the same terms as a majority sale References to "Drag-Along Notice" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

6.1 "Fair Market Value" means value determined by an independent valuer appointed by the Institute of Chartered Accountants in Ireland References to "Fair Market Value" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

7.1 "Founders" means Siobhán O'Connell and Elena Vasquez References to "Founders" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

8.1 "Investor Majority" means Investors holding more than 60% of Investor Shares References to "Investor Majority" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

9.1 "Investor Shares" means Series A Preferred, Series B Preferred and other shares issued to Investors References to "Investor Shares" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

10.1 "Ordinary Shares" means the ordinary shares in the capital of the Company References to "Ordinary Shares" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

11.1 "Reserved Matters" means matters requiring Investor Majority consent as set out in Schedule 3 References to "Reserved Matters" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

12.1 "Tag-Along Notice" means a notice allowing minority shareholders to participate in a majority sale References to "Tag-Along Notice" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

13.1 "Transfer" means any sale, assignment, charge or other disposition References to "Transfer" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2. Interpretation

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (1) headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; (2) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; (3) references to a person include bodies corporate, unincorporated associations and partnerships; (4) "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901); (5) references to statutes or statutory provisions include any subordinate legislation and any modification, consolidation or re-enactment thereof; (6) references to writing include email where acknowledged by the recipient; (7) references to days are to calendar days unless stated as Business Days; (8) Business Day means a day on which banks are open for business in Dublin, Ireland and London, England; (9) any obligation on a party not to do something includes an obligation not to allow it to be done; (10) the words include and including are without limitation.

The Schedules form part of this Agreement and shall have the same force and effect as if set out in the body of this Agreement. In the event of conflict between the body and a Schedule, the body shall prevail unless the Schedule expressly states otherwise.

This Agreement has been negotiated at arm's length. Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice and that the doctrine of contra proferentem shall not apply in the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

3. Share Capital; Classes

The authorised share capital comprises Ordinary Shares, Series A Preferred Shares and Series B Preferred Shares with the rights set out in the Constitution and this Agreement.

Series B Preferred Shares carry a 1x non-participating liquidation preference, anti-dilution protection on a broad-based weighted average basis, and conversion rights into Ordinary Shares on a 1:1 basis subject to adjustments.

A summary cap table as at Completion is set out in Schedule 1.

4. Board Composition; Reserved Matters

The Board shall comprise up to seven (7) directors: (1) two (2) Founder directors appointed by the Founders; (2) two (2) Investor directors appointed by the Investor Majority; (3) up to three (3) independent directors appointed by mutual agreement.

Board meetings require quorum of three directors including at least one Investor director. Board materials shall be circulated at least five Business Days before meetings except in emergencies.

The following Reserved Matters require Investor Majority consent: amendment of constitutional documents adversely affecting Investor Shares; declaration of dividends; incurrence of indebtedness above €500,000; approval of annual budget variances greater than 15%; appointment/removal of CEO or CFO; any material litigation settlement above €250,000; any sale of all or substantially all assets; any IPO or trade sale process.

5. Transfer Restrictions; Pre-emption

No Shareholder may Transfer shares except as permitted under this Agreement. Permitted Transfers include transfers to Affiliates subject to undertaking to be bound by this Agreement.

A Shareholder wishing to Transfer must first offer shares to existing Shareholders pro rata at Fair Market Value (right of first refusal), with 30 days to accept.

Transfers not complying with this clause are void and shall not be registered.

6. Drag-Along; Tag-Along

If holders of 75% of all shares approve a bona fide sale to a third party, they may deliver a Drag-Along Notice requiring all Shareholders to sell on the same terms, subject to customary limitations for dissenting founders regarding representations.

If founders propose a sale of more than 50% of their shares, other Shareholders may deliver a Tag-Along Notice to participate pro rata.

7. Information Rights; Inspection

Investors shall receive quarterly management accounts within 30 days of quarter end, annual audited accounts within 120 days, annual budget, cap table updates, and material litigation notices.

Investors may inspect books and records on reasonable notice during business hours with confidentiality obligations.

8. Non-Compete; Confidentiality

Founders and key employees bound by this Agreement shall not compete with the core business in the EEA for twelve months after ceasing to hold 5% or more of shares, subject to Irish enforceability principles.

All Shareholders shall keep terms and confidential information secret.

9. Termination; Governing Law

This Agreement terminates on IPO or when no Investor holds shares, save for surviving provisions.

Governed by the laws of Ireland. Irish courts have exclusive jurisdiction.

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (1) headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; (2) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; (3) references to a person include bodies corporate, unincorporated associations and partnerships; (4) "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901); (5) references to statutes or statutory provisions include any subordinate legislation and any modification, consolidation or re-enactment thereof; (6) references to writing include email where acknowledged by the recipient; (7) references to days are to calendar days unless stated as Business Days; (8) Business Day means a day on which banks are open for business in Dublin, Ireland and London, England; (9) any obligation on a party not to do something includes an obligation not to allow it to be done; (10) the words include and including are without limitation.

The Schedules form part of this Agreement and shall have the same force and effect as if set out in the body of this Agreement. In the event of conflict between the body and a Schedule, the body shall prevail unless the Schedule expressly states otherwise.

This Agreement has been negotiated at arm's length. Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice and that the doctrine of contra proferentem shall not apply in the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

3. Share Capital; Classes

The authorised share capital comprises Ordinary Shares, Series A Preferred Shares and Series B Preferred Shares with the rights set out in the Constitution and this Agreement.

Series B Preferred Shares carry a 1x non-participating liquidation preference, anti-dilution protection on a broad-based weighted average basis, and conversion rights into Ordinary Shares on a 1:1 basis subject to adjustments.

A summary cap table as at Completion is set out in Schedule 1.

4. Board Composition; Reserved Matters

The Board shall comprise up to seven (7) directors: (1) two (2) Founder directors appointed by the Founders; (2) two (2) Investor directors appointed by the Investor Majority; (3) up to three (3) independent directors appointed by mutual agreement.

Board meetings require quorum of three directors including at least one Investor director. Board materials shall be circulated at least five Business Days before meetings except in emergencies.

The following Reserved Matters require Investor Majority consent: amendment of constitutional documents adversely affecting Investor Shares; declaration of dividends; incurrence of indebtedness above €500,000; approval of annual budget variances greater than 15%; appointment/removal of CEO or CFO; any material litigation settlement above €250,000; any sale of all or substantially all assets; any IPO or trade sale process.

5. Transfer Restrictions; Pre-emption

No Shareholder may Transfer shares except as permitted under this Agreement. Permitted Transfers include transfers to Affiliates subject to undertaking to be bound by this Agreement.

A Shareholder wishing to Transfer must first offer shares to existing Shareholders pro rata at Fair Market Value (right of first refusal), with 30 days to accept.

Transfers not complying with this clause are void and shall not be registered.

6. Drag-Along; Tag-Along

If holders of 75% of all shares approve a bona fide sale to a third party, they may deliver a Drag-Along Notice requiring all Shareholders to sell on the same terms, subject to customary limitations for dissenting founders regarding representations.

If founders propose a sale of more than 50% of their shares, other Shareholders may deliver a Tag-Along Notice to participate pro rata.

7. Information Rights; Inspection

Investors shall receive quarterly management accounts within 30 days of quarter end, annual audited accounts within 120 days, annual budget, cap table updates, and material litigation notices.

Investors may inspect books and records on reasonable notice during business hours with confidentiality obligations.

The Company shall: (1) use commercially reasonable efforts to make the Services available 99.9% of each calendar month, excluding scheduled maintenance notified at least 48 hours in advance; (2) provide the Services in compliance with applicable law, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 and the Data Protection Act 2018; (3) maintain ISO 27001 certification or equivalent security controls during the term; (4) assign qualified personnel with appropriate skills and experience; (5) not materially degrade the overall functionality of the Services during the term.

Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL shall: (i) provide timely access to personnel, systems and information reasonably required; (ii) ensure authorised users comply with acceptable use policies; (iii) not use the Services to store or transmit unlawful content; (iv) maintain appropriate backups of Customer Data where required by law or internal policy.

Unless otherwise agreed in writing, the Company may engage subcontractors provided it remains responsible for their acts and omissions. Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL shall pay the Fees in accordance with the applicable Order Form. Unless stated otherwise, Fees are exclusive of VAT and similar taxes, which shall be added where applicable.

Invoices are issued monthly in arrears and are payable within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower.

If Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL disputes an invoice in good faith, it shall pay the undisputed portion and notify the Company in writing of the dispute within fifteen (15) days, providing reasonable detail.

The Company may suspend Services for non-payment after thirty (30) days' written notice, provided suspension shall not relieve Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL of payment obligations for Services already rendered.

Customer Data remains the property of Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL. Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL grants the Company a licence to process Customer Data solely to provide the Services and as described in the Data Processing Agreement.

Feedback provided by Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL may be used by the Company without restriction or obligation. Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL waives any moral rights in Feedback to the extent permitted by law.

6. Confidentiality

Each party shall keep Confidential Information secret and use it only for purposes of this Agreement. Confidential Information may be disclosed to employees, advisers and contractors on a need-to-know basis subject to equivalent confidentiality obligations.

Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is public through no breach; (b) was lawfully known before disclosure; (c) is independently developed; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, regulation or court order (with notice where permitted).

On termination, each party shall return or destroy Confidential Information within thirty (30) days, except one archival copy may be retained for compliance purposes subject to ongoing confidentiality.

7. Data Protection

To the extent the Company processes personal data on behalf of Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL, the parties shall execute the Data Processing Agreement at Schedule 3, which incorporates Standard Contractual Clauses where required for international transfers.

The Company shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures as described in its security whitepaper and notify Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL without undue delay upon becoming aware of a personal data breach affecting Customer Data.

9. Indemnification

The Company shall indemnify Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL against third-party claims that the Services infringe UK or EU intellectual property rights, subject to exclusions for combinations, modifications, or use outside documentation.

EXCEPT FOR BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, FRAUD OR LIABILITY THAT CANNOT BE LIMITED BY LAW, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID OR PAYABLE IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

11. Term; Termination; Change of Control

This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated. Each Order Form states its initial term and renewal mechanics.

Either party may terminate for material breach not cured within thirty (30) days of written notice. Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL may terminate for convenience on ninety (90) days' notice after the initial term of each Order Form.

Upon termination, access to Services ceases and Fees for committed periods remain payable. The Company shall provide export of Customer Data for thirty (30) days.

Change of Control of Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL does not constitute assignment if the contracting entity remains liable. Change of Control of the Company shall not terminate this Agreement but Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL may negotiate in good faith regarding continuity if the acquirer is a direct competitor.

12. Governing Law; Dispute Resolution

This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction, without prejudice to either party's right to seek injunctive relief in any jurisdiction.

Before commencing proceedings, senior representatives shall meet within fifteen (15) days to attempt good-faith resolution. If unresolved within thirty (30) days, either party may proceed to litigation.

Change of Control of the Company shall not terminate this Agreement but Snowflake Computing UK Ltd may negotiate in good faith regarding continuity if the acquirer is a direct competitor.

This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction, without prejudice to either party's right to seek injunctive relief in any jurisdiction.

NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, GOODWILL OR DATA.

EXCEPT FOR BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, FRAUD OR LIABILITY THAT CANNOT BE LIMITED BY LAW, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID OR PAYABLE IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

11. Term; Termination; Change of Control

This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated. Each Order Form states its initial term and renewal mechanics.

Either party may terminate for material breach not cured within thirty (30) days of written notice. Continental Reinsurance AG may terminate for convenience on ninety (90) days' notice after the initial term of each Order Form.

Upon termination, access to Services ceases and Fees for committed periods remain payable. The Company shall provide export of Customer Data for thirty (30) days.

Change of Control of Continental Reinsurance AG does not constitute assignment if the contracting entity remains liable. Change of Control of the Company shall not terminate this Agreement but Continental Reinsurance AG may negotiate in good faith regarding continuity if the acquirer is a direct competitor.

12. Governing Law; Dispute Resolution

This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction, without prejudice to either party's right to seek injunctive relief in any jurisdiction.

Before commencing proceedings, senior representatives shall meet within fifteen (15) days to attempt good-faith resolution. If unresolved within thirty (30) days, either party may proceed to litigation.

Contract of Employment

Dr. Siobhán O'Connell — Chief Executive Officer

Background

(A) Acme AI Limited is a private company limited by shares incorporated in Ireland (Company Registration No. 678901) whose registered office is at 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland.

(B) The Company carries on the business of developing and licensing artificial intelligence software-as-a-service solutions for enterprise customers in the European Union, United Kingdom and North America.

(C) The Company wishes to employ Dr. Siobhán O'Connell as Chief Executive Officer on the terms set out in this contract of employment.

(D) The parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged), the parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901) References to "Company" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Employee" means Dr. Siobhán O'Connell References to "Employee" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Commencement Date" means the date specified in clause 2 References to "Commencement Date" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

4.1 "Confidential Information" means information relating to the business, customers, technology, finances and affairs of the Company References to "Confidential Information" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

5.1 "Garden Leave" means a period during which the Employee remains employed but is not required to attend work References to "Garden Leave" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

6.1 "Intellectual Property" means all inventions, works, software, data models, algorithms and materials created in connection with employment References to "Intellectual Property" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2. Interpretation

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (1) headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; (2) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; (3) references to a person include bodies corporate, unincorporated associations and partnerships; (4) "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901); (5) references to statutes or statutory provisions include any subordinate legislation and any modification, consolidation or re-enactment thereof; (6) references to writing include email where acknowledged by the recipient; (7) references to days are to calendar days unless stated as Business Days; (8) Business Day means a day on which banks are open for business in Dublin, Ireland and London, England; (9) any obligation on a party not to do something includes an obligation not to allow it to be done; (10) the words include and including are without limitation.

The Schedules form part of this Agreement and shall have the same force and effect as if set out in the body of this Agreement. In the event of conflict between the body and a Schedule, the body shall prevail unless the Schedule expressly states otherwise.

This Agreement has been negotiated at arm's length. Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice and that the doctrine of contra proferentem shall not apply in the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

3. Appointment; Duties

The Company employs the Employee as Chief Executive Officer commencing on 1 March 2022 (or such other date agreed in writing).

The Employee shall devote full professional time and attention to duties, comply with all lawful directions, and act in the best interests of the Company.

The Employee shall not engage in any other business without prior written consent, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld for non-competing advisory roles occupying less than four hours per month.

Place of work is primarily 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland, with hybrid working up to three days remote subject to business needs.

4. Remuneration; Benefits

Base salary of €285,000 per annum, paid monthly in arrears by credit transfer, subject to deduction of income tax, USC and PRSI.

Eligibility for annual discretionary bonus up to 30% of base salary based on Company and individual performance, not contractual unless expressly stated in a bonus plan.

Participation in the Company pension scheme with employer contribution of 6% of pensionable salary, subject to scheme rules.

Twenty-five (25) days' paid annual leave plus public holidays, pro-rated in leave year of commencement.

Private medical insurance through VHI Corporate Plan or equivalent, subject to insurer terms.

5. Confidentiality; Company Property

The Employee shall not use or disclose Confidential Information except in the proper performance of duties or as required by law.

All documents, devices, access credentials and materials remain Company property and shall be returned on demand and on termination.

6. Intellectual Property

The Employee assigns to the Company all Intellectual Property created during employment relating to the business or using Company resources.

The Employee shall execute documents and provide assistance reasonably required to perfect such assignment at the Company's expense.

Moral rights are waived to the fullest extent permitted by the Copyright and Related Rights Act 2000.

7. Restrictive Covenants

For twelve (12) months after termination, the Employee shall not directly compete with the core AI document intelligence product line in the EEA and UK.

For twelve (12) months, the Employee shall not solicit or entice away key employees or customers with whom they had material contact in the twelve months preceding termination.

The Employee acknowledges these restrictions are reasonable and necessary to protect legitimate interests.

8. Termination

During probation (first six months), either party may terminate on one week's notice. Thereafter, three months' written notice is required from either party.

The Company may terminate without notice for gross misconduct including fraud, serious breach of confidentiality, or material dishonesty.

On termination, the Company may place the Employee on Garden Leave for all or part of the notice period.

9. Governing Law

This contract is governed by the laws of Ireland and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Irish courts.

The Terms of Employment (Information) Acts 1994–2014 and Organisation of Working Time Act 1997 apply.

Contract of Employment

Niamh Kelly — Head of Engineering

Background

(A) Acme AI Limited is a private company limited by shares incorporated in Ireland (Company Registration No. 678901) whose registered office is at 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland.

(B) The Company carries on the business of developing and licensing artificial intelligence software-as-a-service solutions for enterprise customers in the European Union, United Kingdom and North America.

(C) The Company wishes to employ Niamh Kelly as Head of Engineering on the terms set out in this contract of employment.

(D) The parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged), the parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901) References to "Company" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Employee" means Niamh Kelly References to "Employee" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Commencement Date" means the date specified in clause 2 References to "Commencement Date" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

4.1 "Confidential Information" means information relating to the business, customers, technology, finances and affairs of the Company References to "Confidential Information" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

5.1 "Garden Leave" means a period during which the Employee remains employed but is not required to attend work References to "Garden Leave" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

6.1 "Intellectual Property" means all inventions, works, software, data models, algorithms and materials created in connection with employment References to "Intellectual Property" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2. Interpretation

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (1) headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; (2) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; (3) references to a person include bodies corporate, unincorporated associations and partnerships; (4) "Company" means Acme AI Limited (Company Registration No. 678901); (5) references to statutes or statutory provisions include any subordinate legislation and any modification, consolidation or re-enactment thereof; (6) references to writing include email where acknowledged by the recipient; (7) references to days are to calendar days unless stated as Business Days; (8) Business Day means a day on which banks are open for business in Dublin, Ireland and London, England; (9) any obligation on a party not to do something includes an obligation not to allow it to be done; (10) the words include and including are without limitation.

The Schedules form part of this Agreement and shall have the same force and effect as if set out in the body of this Agreement. In the event of conflict between the body and a Schedule, the body shall prevail unless the Schedule expressly states otherwise.

This Agreement has been negotiated at arm's length. Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice and that the doctrine of contra proferentem shall not apply in the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

3. Appointment; Duties

The Company employs the Employee as Head of Engineering commencing on 1 March 2022 (or such other date agreed in writing).

The Employee shall devote full professional time and attention to duties, comply with all lawful directions, and act in the best interests of the Company.

The Employee shall not engage in any other business without prior written consent, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld for non-competing advisory roles occupying less than four hours per month.

Place of work is primarily 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland, with hybrid working up to three days remote subject to business needs.

4. Remuneration; Benefits

Base salary of €165,000 per annum, paid monthly in arrears by credit transfer, subject to deduction of income tax, USC and PRSI.

Eligibility for annual discretionary bonus up to 30% of base salary based on Company and individual performance, not contractual unless expressly stated in a bonus plan.

Participation in the Company pension scheme with employer contribution of 6% of pensionable salary, subject to scheme rules.

Twenty-five (25) days' paid annual leave plus public holidays, pro-rated in leave year of commencement.

Private medical insurance through VHI Corporate Plan or equivalent, subject to insurer terms.

5. Confidentiality; Company Property

The Employee shall not use or disclose Confidential Information except in the proper performance of duties or as required by law.

All documents, devices, access credentials and materials remain Company property and shall be returned on demand and on termination.

6. Intellectual Property

The Employee assigns to the Company all Intellectual Property created during employment relating to the business or using Company resources.

The Employee shall execute documents and provide assistance reasonably required to perfect such assignment at the Company's expense.

Moral rights are waived to the fullest extent permitted by the Copyright and Related Rights Act 2000.

7. Restrictive Covenants

For twelve (12) months after termination, the Employee shall not directly compete with the core AI document intelligence product line in the EEA and UK.

For twelve (12) months, the Employee shall not solicit or entice away key employees or customers with whom they had material contact in the twelve months preceding termination.

The Employee acknowledges these restrictions are reasonable and necessary to protect legitimate interests.

8. Termination

During probation (first six months), either party may terminate on one week's notice. Thereafter, three months' written notice is required from either party.

The Company may terminate without notice for gross misconduct including fraud, serious breach of confidentiality, or material dishonesty.

On termination, the Company may place the Employee on Garden Leave for all or part of the notice period.

9. Governing Law

This contract is governed by the laws of Ireland and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Irish courts.

The Terms of Employment (Information) Acts 1994–2014 and Organisation of Working Time Act 1997 apply.

For twelve (12) months, the Employee shall not solicit or entice away key employees or customers with whom they had material contact in the twelve months preceding termination.

The Employee acknowledges these restrictions are reasonable and necessary to protect legitimate interests.

8. Termination

During probation (first six months), either party may terminate on one week's notice. Thereafter, three months' written notice is required from either party.

The Company may terminate without notice for gross misconduct including fraud, serious breach of confidentiality, or material dishonesty.

On termination, the Company may place the Employee on Garden Leave for all or part of the notice period.

9. Governing Law

This contract is governed by the laws of Ireland and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Irish courts.

The Terms of Employment (Information) Acts 1994–2014 and Organisation of Working Time Act 1997 apply.

Execution

Signed by the Employee: Aoife Ryan

Signed for the Company: Dr. Siobhán O'Connell, CEO

Acme AI Limited — Privacy Policy (EU/UK)

Last updated: 1 January 2025. Controller: Acme AI Limited, 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland. Data Protection Officer: privacy@acmeai.example.

This policy explains how we collect, use, share and protect personal data when you use our SaaS platform, website and related services.

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Personal Data" means information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person References to "Personal Data" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Processing" means any operation performed on personal data References to "Processing" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Controller" means entity determining purposes and means of processing References to "Controller" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

4.1 "Processor" means entity processing on behalf of controller References to "Processor" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

5.1 "Special Categories" means data revealing racial origin, health, biometric data for identification, etc. References to "Special Categories" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3. Data We Collect

We may collect: (1) account registration data (name, email, employer, role); (2) usage and telemetry data (IP address, device identifiers, feature usage, logs); (3) customer content uploaded to the platform for document analysis; (4) billing and payment records; (5) support communications; (6) marketing preferences where consent obtained.

4. Legal Bases (GDPR Article 6)

Contract performance for providing the Services; legitimate interests for security, product improvement and fraud prevention balanced against rights; consent for non-essential cookies and marketing; legal obligation for tax and regulatory records.

Remote Working Policy

Hybrid policy up to 3 days remote.

ABC Policy

Zero tolerance. Gifts over €50 require approval.

Whistleblowing

Protected Disclosures Act 2014 procedures.

Pension

6% employer contribution.

Bonus Plan

Discretionary up to 30% base.

Acme AI Limited — Privacy Policy (EU/UK)

Last updated: 1 January 2025. Controller: Acme AI Limited, 3rd Floor, The Merchants Building, Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Dublin 2, D02 X576, Ireland. Data Protection Officer: privacy@acmeai.example.

This policy explains how we collect, use, share and protect personal data when you use our SaaS platform, website and related services.

1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 "Personal Data" means information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person References to "Personal Data" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

2.1 "Processing" means any operation performed on personal data References to "Processing" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3.1 "Controller" means entity determining purposes and means of processing References to "Controller" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

4.1 "Processor" means entity processing on behalf of controller References to "Processor" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

5.1 "Special Categories" means data revealing racial origin, health, biometric data for identification, etc. References to "Special Categories" include, where the context permits, any successor or assignee thereof.

3. Data We Collect

We may collect: (1) account registration data (name, email, employer, role); (2) usage and telemetry data (IP address, device identifiers, feature usage, logs); (3) customer content uploaded to the platform for document analysis; (4) billing and payment records; (5) support communications; (6) marketing preferences where consent obtained.

4. Legal Bases (GDPR Article 6)

Contract performance for providing the Services; legitimate interests for security, product improvement and fraud prevention balanced against rights; consent for non-essential cookies and marketing; legal obligation for tax and regulatory records.

5. International Transfers

Where data is transferred outside the EEA/UK, we implement Standard Contractual Clauses (2021/914) and supplementary measures including encryption and access controls.

6. Retention; Rights

Data retained for the subscription term plus up to seven years for legal claims and tax. Data subjects may access, rectify, erase, restrict, port data, and object. Complaints may be lodged with the Irish Data Protection Commission.

7. Security; Breach Notification

We maintain ISO 27001-aligned controls, annual penetration testing, and staff training. Personal data breaches are notified to supervisory authorities within 72 hours where required and to customers without undue delay.

6. Confidentiality

Each party shall keep Confidential Information secret and use it only for purposes of this Agreement. Confidential Information may be disclosed to employees, advisers and contractors on a need-to-know basis subject to equivalent confidentiality obligations.

Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is public through no breach; (b) was lawfully known before disclosure; (c) is independently developed; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, regulation or court order (with notice where permitted).

On termination, each party shall return or destroy Confidential Information within thirty (30) days, except one archival copy may be retained for compliance purposes subject to ongoing confidentiality.

7. Data Protection

To the extent the Company processes personal data on behalf of Customer Template, the parties shall execute the Data Processing Agreement at Schedule 3, which incorporates Standard Contractual Clauses where required for international transfers.

The Company shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures as described in its security whitepaper and notify Customer Template without undue delay upon becoming aware of a personal data breach affecting Customer Data.
